description_long: This object is a hand tool with a robust, dark metallic head and a single, sharply curved blade extending from one side. The blade is thick and tapers to a pointed edge, suitable for prying or splitting tasks, and the opposite side of the head is flat, functioning as a hammer or striking surface. The handle is made of wood, featuring a natural brown color and a gentle curve for ergonomic grip, with a smooth finish and visible wood grain. The head is securely mounted onto the handle, with a rounded wooden wedge visible at the top, indicating a traditional assembly method. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements or branding, and the tool appears well-suited for heavy-duty urban tasks such as demolition or landscaping. The materials and construction suggest durability and effective force transmission during use.
description: A heavy-duty axe with a curved wooden handle and a thick, pointed metal blade, featuring a flat hammer end and a traditional wedged mounting.
description_view_0: Front view shows the broad, flat hammer end of the axe head and the rounded top of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the pronounced curve of the handle and the sharply pointed, elongated blade extending horizontally.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the axe head and the full length of the handle, emphasizing its curvature and wood grain.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the blade's profile and the secure mounting of the head to the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a smooth finish, oval cross-section, ergonomic grip, and visible wood grain.
attribute_blade_design: Single, thick, pointed blade with a flat hammer end; no beard, decorative, or secondary edges.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with a visible wooden wedge securing the head.
